tests.response: Stack frame: sub sp, sp, 0x2f8 (760 bytes). Function signature: (int32_t arg1, int32_t arg2). 125 stack variables (sp[0x4..0x308]). Saves {r4, r5, fp, lr} on entry.

tests.description: Second strcpy in formSetFirewallCfg — copies getWispIfName result to var_298h
tests.response: At 0xaddac: getWispIfName() returns interface name into var_2ch. At 0xadde4: strcpy(var_298h, var_2ch) — copies to stack. var_298h then used in doSystemCmd with iptables format strings. Source is getWispIfName() (internal function, not direct user input). However, if getWispIfName returns attacker-controlled data, command injection via iptables format string possible.

Stack frame: sub sp, sp, 0x7c (124 bytes). Calls: fcn.0002bd4c (websGetVar) x1, GetValue x2, strcmp, printf, set_wl_guest_qos_list, CommitCfm, doSystemCmd, sprintf. One sprintf at 0x63718 writes result code to stack buffer s (fp-0x38). sprintf format: '{"errCode":"%d"}' with var_10h — safe (integer only).

tests.description: doSystemCmd in formSetQosBand — fixed format string
tests.response: The doSystemCmd call at 0x636fc uses hardcoded integer arguments (15, 6). No user-controlled string data reaches doSystemCmd. Safe.

Stack frame: sub sp, sp, 0x3180 + 0x30 = 0x31B0 (12720 bytes). HUGE stack frame. Saves {r4, r5, r6, r7, fp, lr}. websGetVar('list', default) at 0xb89e4. Multiple large memset calls: 0x200, 0x1c0, 0x1000, 0x200, 0x1000, 0x400, 0x200, 0x40, 0x200 bytes. Contains sprintf, strcpy, strcat, strcmp calls.

Stack frame: large. 10+ websGetVar calls. Parameters: wrlEn, hideSsid, ssid, security, wrlPwd, wrlEn_5g, hideSsid_5g, ssid_5g, security_5g, wrlPwd_5g (and more). 2 strcpy calls, many SetValue calls, 2 GetValue, multiple strlen checks.

Stack frame: large. Primarily a getter/status function. Uses malloc for buffer allocation. Contains: sprintf x6, GetValue x10+, strcmp x10+, printf x12+, memcpy x5, strcat x1, doSystemCmd x1, getLanIfName, getIfIp. No websGetVar calls (getter function — reads config, doesn't write).

Stack frame: massive (10060 bytes function). Pure getter function — reads system status. Contains: snprintf x2 (safe!), GetValue x20+, strcmp x20+, printf x20+, memcpy x10+, atoi x5, memset x15+. Uses snprintf (bounded) instead of sprintf in some places. No websGetVar calls. No strcpy. No doSystemCmd with user input.
Stack frame: large. Port status getter. Contains: GetValue x10+, strcpy x9, memcpy x14, memset x10+, strcmp x3, strlen, atoi. No websGetVar calls.

Stack frame: sub sp, sp, 0x2c (44 bytes). Small function. NO websGetVar calls — no user input processing. Calls get_eth_name, getIfIp, then 11 doSystemCmd calls with HARDCODED strings. Kills processes, flushes iptables, configures switch registers.

Stack frame: sub sp, sp, 0x1c (28 bytes). Very small function. websGetVar('mac', '00:01:02:11:22:33') at 0x4560c. doSystemCmd('cfm mac %s', mac_value) at 0x45638. DIRECT command injection path: user input → format string → system command.

Stack frame: sub sp, sp, 0x74 (116 bytes). websGetVar calls: mit_rate, PPPOEName, PPPOEPassword, mit_wrlpwd, mit_linktype. Uses SetValue extensively (15+ calls). One doSystemCmd call. No strcpy. No sprintf with user input.

tests.request: r2 -c 'aaa; s sym.R7WebsSecurityHandler; pdf' — search for goform endpoints
tests.response:
R7WebsSecurityHandler (5280 bytes at 0x2f500) contains whitelist of URLs that bypass authentication. Whitelisted goform endpoints found:
  /goform/getRebootStatus (0xdf4fc)
  /goform/telnet (0xdf540) — CRITICAL: telnet access without auth!
  /goform/ate (0xdf550) — ATE test without auth
  /goform/GetRouterStatus (0xdf5a4) — info disclosure
  /goform/getWanParameters (0xdf5bc)
  /goform/GetUSBStatus (0xdf5d8)
  /goform/WifiApScan (0xdf640)
These endpoints can be accessed without any authentication cookie.

findings.title: Stack Buffer Overflow in formSetFirewallCfg via 'firewallEn' parameter (strcpy)

details.sink: strcpy(dest, src) at 0x000ad87c — dest is stack buffer at fp-0x34
details.buffer_size: ~4 bytes (dest from fp-0x34, next var var_30h at fp-0x30)
details.overflow_to_lr: 0x38 (56 bytes) from buffer start to saved LR
data_flow: 0xad850: fcn.0002bd4c(connId, 'firewallEn', '1111') -> returns user input pointer
0xad854: str r0, [src] (fp-0x1c) — stores input pointer
0xad858: strlen(src) -> r3
0xad864: if strlen <= 3, skip (goes to 0xae048)
0xad86c: ldr r3, [src] — load input pointer
0xad870: sub r2, dest — r2 = fp-0x34 (destination buffer)
0xad87c: strcpy(fp-0x34, user_input) — NO BOUNDS CHECK
post_overflow_impact: After strcpy, code reads individual chars: dest[0] (fp-0x34), dest[2] (fp-0x32), dest[1] (fp-0x33)
Then uses sprintf at 0xad8d8 with format '%c,1500;%c,1500;%c,1500' into var_fch (64 bytes at fp-0xfc)
Overflow overwrites var_30h, var_2ch, var_28h, var_24h, var_20h, var_1ch (src pointer itself!), var_18h, var_14h, var_10h, fp, lr
Control of LR = arbitrary code execution (no canary, no PIE)
details.mitigations: NONE (No canary, No PIE, No RELRO, No FORTIFY)
details.auth_notes: Not in R7WebsSecurityHandler whitelist — requires valid session cookie
details.related_cve: CVE-2024-40891 (Zyxel, similar pattern; this is a DIFFERENT vendor/product)
details.exploitability: Trivial — 56 bytes to overwrite saved LR, no mitigations, fixed binary base

findings.title: Potential Command Injection via iptables format strings in formSetFirewallCfg

doSystemCmd_calls: 0xadf4c-0xae024: Multiple additional iptables doSystemCmd calls with var_298h and var_2a8h
details.source_of_format_arg: var_298h populated from strcpy(var_298h, getWispIfName()) at 0xadde4
details.risk: getWispIfName() returns interface name from config — if config is corrupted or WISP interface name is user-controlled, command injection is possible

websGetVar_params: (parameter name at computed address — likely band config)

dangerous_sinks.sprintf: 1 call at 0x63718 — writes integer error code, safe

details.assessment: No user-controlled data reaches dangerous sinks without sanitization

findings.title: Stack Buffer Overflow in formSetPPTPUserList via sprintf with user data

vulnerable_instructions: 0xb8b58: sprintf(s, '%s;%s;%s;%s;%s', parsed_fields...) — writes to stack buffer indexed by loop counter * 0x200
0xb8dc8: sprintf(s, '%d;%d;%s;%s;%s;%s;%s;%s', ...) — writes to var_610h area (0x200 bytes)
0xb8e50: sprintf(s, '%s;%s;%s;%s;%s', ...) — another large format into stack
data_flow: User input 'list' parameter parsed with delimiter '~' (strchr at 0xb8c84)
Each segment parsed into struct fields via fcn.0xb7cfc and fcn.0xb7da4
Fields at offsets 0x00, 0x40, 0x80, 0xc0, 0x100, 0x140, 0x180 in parsed struct
sprintf at 0xb8b58 concatenates all fields with '%s;%s;%s;%s;%s' format
Destination buffer is at loop_counter * 0x200 offset from stack base
details.loop_info: Loop runs up to 8 iterations (cmp r3, 7 at 0xb8bd8)
details.risk: If any parsed field exceeds 64 bytes, sprintf overflows the 512-byte per-entry buffer. Multiple %s concatenation without bounds checking.

vulnerable_instructions: 0xb8b98: strcpy(dest, src+0x80) — copies parsed username to array entry (64-byte dest, unbounded src)
0xb8f08: strcpy(dest, parsed_str) — copies parsed struct data into entry array
strcpy_contexts: At 0xb8b98: dest = entry_array[dest_counter * 64], src = parsed_struct + 0x80 (username field). dest_counter incremented, entry used for comparison later.
At 0xb8f08: dest = entry_array[dest_counter * 64], src = parsed_struct base. This is in the user-matching branch — copies current entry data.
details.buffer_size: 64 bytes per entry in comparison array (shifted left by 6 = *64)
details.risk: Username/password fields from user input parsed without length validation, then strcpy'd into 64-byte stack slots

details.vulnerable_instruction: 0xb939c: strcat(s1, s2) — appends data without bounds check
details.context: In the PPTP user config serialization loop, strcat appends entries to an output buffer. If many entries with long fields, buffer overflow occurs.

findings.title: Stack Buffer Overflow in formWifiBasicSet via 'wrlPwd'/'wrlPwd_5g' parameters (strcpy)

vulnerable_instructions: 0x929ec: strcpy(dest, src) — dest at fp-0x9d (var 'dest'), src from websGetVar 'wrlPwd'
0x92d60: strcpy(s, src) — dest at fp-0x5d (var 's'), src from websGetVar 'wrlPwd_5g'
data_flow_wrlPwd: 0x92388: websGetVar(connId, 'wrlPwd', '12345678') -> stored as var 'src' at fp-0x2c
data_flow_wrlPwd_5g: Similar flow: websGetVar 'wrlPwd_5g' -> var_1ch at fp-0x1c
details.context: After strcpy, the code reads individual characters for WPA PSK config processing. The passwords are used with SetValue to configure wl.0.s.wpa_psk.pass and wl.5.s.wpa_psk.pass.
details.buffer_analysis: dest at fp-0x9d and s at fp-0x5d are small stack buffers, likely sized for typical WPA passwords (8-63 chars). Overflow with long password string corrupts stack.

findings.title: Auth Bypass — formGetRouterStatus accessible without authentication

details.evidence: R7WebsSecurityHandler at 0x2f500 contains whitelist entry: strncmp(url, '/goform/GetRouterStatus', 23) at ~0x2fd00. String at 0xdf5a4: '/goform/GetRouterStatus'
information_leaked: LAN IP address and network info
details.risk: Pre-auth information disclosure of router network configuration

dangerous_sinks.sprintf: 6 calls — write config data to heap-allocated response buffer (malloc'd)
dangerous_sinks.doSystemCmd: 1 call at 0x5e69c — with format from get_proc_pid, not user-controlled

details.assessment: No direct stack buffer overflow from user input (no websGetVar), but information disclosure via unauthenticated access is significant.

findings.title: formGetSystemStatus — Uses snprintf, no exploitable overflow found

details.assessment: Largest handler analyzed. Uses snprintf at 0x594a4 and 0x5991c (bounded writes). No websGetVar calls — pure status getter. No strcpy/sprintf with user input. Reads config via GetValue and formats output via printf. Relatively safe despite size.
safe_patterns: GetValue reads from config store, not from HTTP request

findings.title: formGetPortStatus — 9 strcpy calls but source is internal (GetValue/tpi functions)

strcpy_calls: 0x5791c, 0x57944, 0x5796c, 0x57994, 0x579bc — 5 strcpy calls copying WiFi status info
details.source_analysis: All strcpy sources come from GetValue() (config store reads) or tpi_wifi_get_status/tpi_wifi_get_ip_info (internal APIs). No direct user input via websGetVar. Risk: if config store contains oversized values, overflow possible.
details.assessment: Low direct risk — no user input paths. Internal config corruption could potentially trigger overflow via GetValue -> strcpy chain.

findings.title: Manufacturing Test Mode — No authentication, destructive operations via doSystemCmd

details.risk: Triggers manufacturing test mode — kills all network daemons, flushes iptables NAT rules, reconfigures hardware switch registers. Effectively a denial-of-service that disrupts all router networking. Response: 'start mfg mode success.'
details.authentication_required: Unknown — needs dynamic verification. Not in R7WebsSecurityHandler whitelist, so likely requires auth. However, Tenda routers have historically had auth bypass issues.
details.no_command_injection: All doSystemCmd strings are hardcoded — no injection possible

findings.title: Command Injection in formWriteFacMac via 'mac' parameter (doSystemCmd)

data_flow: 0x455f0: ldr r0, [var_18h] — load connection handle (arg1)
0x455f4-0x45608: load 'mac' string and '00:01:02:11:22:33' default
0x4560c: fcn.0002bd4c(connId, 'mac', '00:01:02:11:22:33') — websGetVar
0x45610: str r0, [var_10h] — store user input pointer
0x4562c: load format string 'cfm mac %s' into r3
0x45634: ldr r1, [var_10h] — load user mac value as arg
0x45638: doSystemCmd('cfm mac %s', user_mac) — COMMAND INJECTION
details.exploitation: Send mac=;id or mac=`id` or mac=$(whoami) to execute arbitrary commands. The 'cfm mac %s' format passes user input directly to system()/popen(). No sanitization, no validation of MAC format.
details.auth_notes: Not in R7WebsSecurityHandler whitelist — requires session. However, this is a factory/manufacturing endpoint that may have reduced auth.
details.impact: Full remote code execution as root (httpd runs as root on Tenda routers)

findings.title: formQuickIndex — User credentials passed directly to SetValue without sanitization

details.doSystemCmd_call: 0x50438: doSystemCmd('cfm post multiWAN ManualDown1\n') — hardcoded, safe
details.additional_doSystemCmd: 0x50664: doSystemCmd('cfm post multiWAN ManualDown1') — hardcoded, safe
data_flow: PPPOEPassword -> stored via SetValue to 'wan1.ppoe.password' (config key)
mit_wrlpwd -> stored via SetValue to 'wl.0.s.wpa_psk.pass' and 'wl.5.s.wpa_psk.pass'
PPPOEName -> stored via SetValue to PPPoE config
details.risk: No direct overflow or injection. User credentials stored in plaintext config. The passwords are passed to SetValue with SetValue handling the storage. If SetValue has internal buffer overflows, this could be exploitable.
details.assessment: No direct memory corruption in this function. Passwords stored in plaintext (CWE-256). Thread created with delay_restart_multiWAN callback.

findings.title: Authentication Bypass — Multiple goform endpoints accessible without auth

details.critical_bypass: /goform/telnet allows unauthenticated activation of telnet daemon, providing root shell access to the router. This is the most severe auth bypass as it provides direct OS-level access.
details.risk: Pre-authentication access to sensitive router functions and information. The /goform/telnet endpoint is particularly dangerous as it enables remote root access without any credentials.

anomalies.description: formSetFirewallCfg contains 9+ doSystemCmd calls with iptables commands using %s format. While the immediate source is getWispIfName() (internal), if the WISP interface name can be influenced through other config endpoints, this becomes a command injection chain.

formSetPPTPUserList has a 12KB+ stack frame (0x31B0 bytes). This is unusually large and suggests complex parsing logic with multiple large buffers. The sprintf calls use multi-field format strings ('%s;%s;%s;%s;%s' and '%d;%d;%s;%s;%s;%s;%s;%s') that concatenate parsed user input fields — classic recipe for buffer overflow.
formMfgTest has NO input validation at all — no websGetVar. It simply executes a sequence of killall and system commands. This is a pure DoS endpoint if accessible.

The R7WebsSecurityHandler whitelist uses strncmp with exact lengths, suggesting the whitelist was intentionally designed. The inclusion of /goform/telnet is likely a development/factory backdoor that was never removed in production firmware.

fcn.0002bd4c (websGetVar wrapper) is called extensively across all form handlers. It takes 3 args: (connId, paramName, defaultValue). The return value is a pointer to the HTTP POST parameter value, which can be arbitrarily long. Every function that passes this pointer to strcpy/sprintf without length check is vulnerable.
